description: Worktree provisioning benchmark: baseline vs CoW vs sparse-checkout

note.baseline: git worktree add only — no extra steps
note.cow: git worktree add + copy-on-write copy of node_modules (--reflink=auto on Linux, -cR on macOS)
note.sparse: git worktree add + sparse-checkout cone mode limited to 'packages/cleo'
note.disk_bytes: du -sb of the worktree directory; -1 means du failed; CoW bytes may appear larger than baseline because the copy is counted separately by du even when reflinks share blocks
